writeup: June 1, 2026 marked a historic day for the Buffalo Bills franchise as the team received the keys to their brand-new Highmark Stadium, with a temporary certificate of occupancy officially issued and the moving process set to begin. The moment represented the culmination of years of planning and investment, signaling that the Bills' long-awaited home was finally becoming a reality. Meanwhile, the NFL world was buzzing over the blockbuster trade sending Myles Garrett to the Los Angeles Rams, a move that directly affects the Bills' 2026 schedule and the broader AFC/NFC competitive landscape. Analysts at ESPN and Buffalo-focused outlets wasted no time breaking down what Garrett's new home means for primetime matchups and championship contention. With June 1 also serving as a traditional roster and trade deadline marker, Bills fans and media alike were already looking ahead to potential trade targets the team should pursue before training camp opens.
